name: V-Neck
description: A neckline that dips down in a V shape at the front, elongating the neck and flattering most body types

name: Round Neck
description: A classic circular neckline that sits close to the base of the neck

name: Off-Shoulder
description: A neckline that sits below the shoulders, exposing the collarbone and shoulders

name: Sweetheart
description: A heart-shaped neckline that dips in the center, creating a romantic silhouette

name: Square Neck
description: A straight-across neckline with right-angle corners, creating a structured look

name: Boat Neck
description: A wide neckline that runs horizontally across the collarbone, nearly reaching the shoulder points

name: Halter Neck
description: A neckline with straps that tie or fasten behind the neck, leaving shoulders and back exposed

name: Cowl Neck
description: A draped, fabric-folded neckline that creates soft, flowing folds

name: Scoop Neck
description: A deep, U-shaped neckline that scoops lower than a round neck

name: Illusion Neck
description: A sheer mesh or lace panel over the chest area, creating the illusion of bare skin while providing coverage

name: Keyhole Neck
description: A round neckline with a small cutout (keyhole) at the front or back

name: One-Shoulder
description: An asymmetric neckline with one strap, leaving one shoulder bare

name: Short Sleeve
description: Sleeves ending above the elbow, typically at mid-upper arm

name: Long Sleeve
description: Sleeves extending to the wrist

name: 3/4 Sleeve
description: Sleeves ending between the elbow and wrist

name: Puff Sleeve
description: Gathered sleeves that balloon out before tapering at the cuff or shoulder

name: Bell Sleeve
description: Sleeves that flare out from the elbow like a bell

name: Cap Sleeve
description: Very short sleeves that just cover the shoulder cap

name: Sleeveless
description: No sleeves — the garment ends at the shoulder seam

name: Flutter Sleeve
description: Short, loose sleeves that drape softly and flutter with movement

name: Raglan Sleeve
description: Sleeves that extend in one piece from the collar, with diagonal seam from underarm to collarbone

name: Kimono Sleeve
description: Wide, straight sleeves cut as one piece with the body of the garment

name: Mandarin Collar
description: A short, stand-up collar that does not fold over, originating from Chinese traditional wear

name: Peter Pan Collar
description: A flat, rounded collar lying flat against the garment

name: Notch Collar
description: A tailored collar with a V-shaped notch where the collar meets the lapel

name: Stand Collar
description: A tall, upright collar that stands against the neck

name: Shawl Collar
description: A rounded collar that rolls softly from the neckline without a notch

name: Spread Collar
description: A wide collar with points spread apart, commonly used in dress shirts

name: A-Line Skirt
description: A skirt that is fitted at the hips and gradually flares out toward the hem, resembling the letter A

name: Pencil Skirt
description: A slim-fitting skirt with a straight, narrow cut

name: Mermaid Skirt
description: A fitted skirt that flares out dramatically at or below the knee, resembling a mermaid tail

name: Flared Skirt
description: A skirt that flares outward from the waist, creating a full, circular silhouette

name: Pleated Skirt
description: A skirt featuring evenly spaced folds (pleats) for added volume and movement

name: Wrap Skirt
description: A skirt that wraps around the body and ties or buttons at the side

name: High-Low Skirt
description: A skirt with a shorter front hemline and a longer back hemline

name: Peplum Skirt
description: A skirt with a short, flared ruffle attached at the waistline

name: Straight Trouser
description: Trousers with a straight, consistent width from hip to hem

name: Wide-Leg Trouser
description: Trousers that widen from the waist or hip to create a flowing, relaxed silhouette

name: Palazzo Trouser
description: Very wide-legged trousers that flow dramatically from the waist

name: Tapered Trouser
description: Trousers that narrow gradually from the hip to the ankle

name: Flared Trouser
description: Trousers that fit snugly through the thigh and flare out from the knee

name: Agbada
description: A flowing wide-sleeved robe worn over an inner shirt (Buba) and trousers (Sokoto). Traditional Yoruba formal attire

name: Kaftan
description: A long, loose garment with wide sleeves, worn as a flowing dress or robe

name: Buba & Iro
description: Traditional Yoruba two-piece outfit — a loose blouse (Buba) paired with a wrapper (Iro) tied around the waist

name: Senator / Safari
description: A structured tunic top with Mandarin collar, typically worn with matching trousers. Popular Nigerian male formal wear

name: Dashiki
description: A colorful, loose-fitting garment with an ornate V-shaped collar and embroidery on the chest

name: Jumpsuit
description: A one-piece garment combining a top and trousers

name: Aso Oke Set
description: Hand-woven cloth from Yorubaland, worn as a complete set — Aso Oke, Gele (headtie), and Ipele (shoulder piece)

name: Boubou
description: A wide, flowing robe with elaborate embroidery, common across West Africa

name: Wrapper Dress
description: A fitted dress made from traditional African print fabric (Ankara, Adire), often with modern styling elements
